title: relay.rs uses default reqwest client (redirects enabled), bypassing redirect-disabled SSRF mitigation
description: relay::add() and relay::remove() call reqwest::Client::new() which uses the default redirect policy (follows up to 10 redirects). The SSRF guard calls is_private_host_resolved() pre-request, but if the relay URL redirects to an internal host (e.g., 192.168.x.x or 169.254.169.254), the redirect is followed after the guard has already passed. The main server http_client is built with redirect::Policy::none() specifically to prevent this, but relay.rs creates its own client and does not apply that setting. An operator who runs 'broadside relay add https://attacker.example/' where attacker.example returns a 301 to http://192.168.1.1/admin would cause the Follow request body to be POSTed to the internal host.
close_reason: Fixed: relay.rs uses no-redirect client with 30s timeout.
